sector.name: Affordable Housing
sector.description: Housing financiers serving underpenetrated affordable home loan markets

fyPerformance: FY26 AUM stood at ₹30,571 Cr (+20% YoY); disbursement stood at ₹9,556 Cr (+17% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹1,096 Cr (+20% YoY)
FY26 ROA stood at 4.4% and ROE stood at 15.9%, excluding labour law impact
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹3,087 Cr (+20% YoY); Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹311 Cr (+27% YoY)
highlights: Branch network stood at 626 branches across 22 states and UTs
Home loans form 73% of AUM, while LAP loans form 27%
Salaried borrowers form 55% of AUM and self-employed borrowers form 45%
Productivity and efficiency improvement are expected to drive another 50 bps improvement in cost-to-income
Company continues to focus on low-ticket affordable housing and underpenetrated geographies

fyPerformance: FY26 AUM stood at ₹23,450 Cr (+15% YoY); disbursement stood at ₹6,780 Cr (+11% YoY)
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹2,350 Cr (+16% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹182 Cr (+18% YoY)
Q4FY26 ROA improved 13 bps QoQ to 3.5%, while ROE improved 38 bps QoQ to 14.7%
highlights: Asset quality remains near historical best levels
Branch network stood at 435 branches across 15 states
CVC Capital Partners became the new promoter
Rating outlook was upgraded to positive by both ICRA and CARE
Management's long-term goal remains to consistently deliver 20%+ AUM growth

fyPerformance: FY26 AUM stood at ₹13,107 Cr (+21% YoY); disbursement stood at ₹4,009 Cr (+11% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹943 Cr (+26% YoY)
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹1,242 Cr (+17% YoY and +21% QoQ)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹261 Cr (+26% YoY), translating into ROA of 8.2% and ROE of 21.2%
highlights: Company has discontinued sanctions below ₹7 lakh to improve customer quality, which also impacted growth in FY26
Branch network stood at 339 branches after adding 39 branches in FY26
Maharashtra and Odisha are showing encouraging early traction
Collection efficiency improved to 100.5% vs 99.1% QoQ, helping 30+ DPD improve to 6.21% vs 6.48% QoQ
Higher ticket size strategy is driven by inflation and better borrower quality, with management aiming to increase ATS by around ₹1 lakh every year

fyPerformance: FY26 AUM stood at ₹15,878 Cr (+25% YoY); disbursement stood at ₹5,424 Cr (+13% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹540 Cr (+41% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹149 Cr (+43% YoY)
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹1,572 Cr (+24% YoY and +19% QoQ)
FY26 ROE stood at 15.7%; adjusted for QIP, ROE stood at 16.8%
highlights: Credit cost stood at 40 bps for FY26
Asset quality improved meaningfully in Q4, with 1+ DPD at 4.7%, down 60 bps QoQ
Gross Stage 3 improved to 1.8%, down 20 bps QoQ
Branch network stood at 171 branches and 373 touch points
AUM per branch, disbursement per branch and AUM per employee are expected to gradually improve with scale and technology

fyPerformance: FY26 gross AUM stood at ₹11,044 Cr (+29% YoY); disbursement stood at ₹3,834 Cr (+14% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹503 Cr (+33% YoY)
Q4FY26 disbursements crossed ₹1,000 Cr for the first time
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹138 Cr (+27% YoY)
Credit cost stood at 0.5% for FY26, in line with guidance of 40-50 bps
highlights: Asset quality improved sharply with 30+ DPD down 100 bps QoQ to 4%
Gross Stage 3 improved by 29 bps QoQ to 1.2%
Management expects FY27 disbursement growth to remain above 20%, with scope to accelerate once macro environment improves
Home loan and LAP disbursement mix remains around 56-57% home loans and balance LAP, with no major change expected in FY27
Affordable housing finance remains one of the stronger lending pockets, supported by end-user demand and underpenetrated markets
